We first compute the total number of armature conductors: 6 poles × 12 slots/pole × 4 conductors/slot = 288 conductors. For a Y-connected, three-phase alternator, the RMS line-to-neutral voltage is given by \( E_{\text{ph}} = 4.44 \, k_w \, f \, N_{\text{ph}} \, \Phi \), where \( N_{\text{ph}} \) is series turns per phase (288/6 = 48 turns per phase for Y), \( f = 60 \) Hz, \( \Phi = 0.025 \) Wb, and the winding factor \( k_w = k_p \times k_d \).
